programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Life in the beautiful Cornish village of Portwenn has become ever more infuriating for Dr Martin Ellingham since his and Louisa's heart-rending decision to call off their wedding. Louisa has left the village, and Martin has begun to question what keeps him in Cornwall. It's certainly not his relentlessly annoying patients. Mrs Tishell's husband, Clive, returns to his wife and concerns the doctor with his lack of hearing. Martin's ex-girlfriend Dr Edith Montgomery aids a patient of his, and the pair immediately come to a disagreement over the patient's need for an operation. Louisa returns with surprise news.

programme.title: Location Location Location
programme.desc: This time, we're catching up with two sets of house hunters whose searches were all about family. In 2018, retired couple Sue and Tom made the difficult decision to sell their beloved country cottage in Tom's native Yorkshire and move south to be closer to Sue's grandson Freddie. After their house sold in just six days, they found themselves living in their motorhome- and winter was coming, Phil took them on a voyage of discovery through the east of England, where it was as much about finding the perfect garden as the perfect house. Two years later, Phil has returned to see whether their relocation reaped rewards. In 2019, recently divorced dad Charlie was back living with his parents whilst he searched for a new home near Maidstone. Keen to provide space and stability for his children Rosie and Hugo, green fingered Charlie was looking for a house with a large garden - but a tight budget and stock-poor search area was making his search nigh on impossible. Having lost out on several properties, Charlie was all at sea, and needed Kirstie's help to find safe harbour. A year on, we're back to see whether his family found dry land.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location Australia
programme.desc: Veronica and Bryce are up to their necks in nappies, finding homes for two very different families in two different states. Bryce is on the hunt for a couple of urban hippies in Noosa's spectacular hinterland. Chad and Tahlia are loved-up first time buyers looking for a tree-change so baby daughter Venice can be closer to her grandparents. Problem is, it's got to have the right 'vibe' and Bryce has got to dig deep to find his inner hippie! At the opposite end of the spectrum, Veronica's on a multi-million dollar hunt on Sydney's exclusive lower North Shore. She's trying to find the dream home for a blended family of seven. But big budgets mean big wish lists and like kids in a candy store, this couple wants more, more, more!

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Steven and Theresa own a dated six-bedroom home which has been on the market for three years with three agents. They've lowered the price from $680,000 to $570,000 and can't really go lower. The market is flooded with more contemporary homes, meaning their house isn't currently very appealing to buyers.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: This is an incredibly dated 1970s four-bed home on an elevated block with a steep driveway. This lovely family with two kids are preparing to downsize, but they seem slightly clueless as to why their house won't sell, and it's scaring off the buyers. It's been on the market for 14 months with a hopeless agent that they have replaced. They'll need to lower their expectations as they're currently asking well over the suburb average, even before the team renovates.

programme.title: Love It Or List It Australia

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: This episode, meet newlyweds Nikki and Andy. Having merged houses a few years ago, Nikki has grown to hate their three-bed terrace but Andy is not for moving.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil are back with the show that unites warring households across the UK by answering that age-old question: should you stay, or should you go? This episode, they meet the Lindleys.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: New Zealand's Best Homes
programme.desc: Phil makes his way to an island paradise to visit a unique beach side home and travels to Queenstown for the breathtaking views from a home made of copper.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Tani Richards has some fascinating pastimes. As well as practising yoga and horse riding, she has a passion for all things medieval, including dressing up like Maid Marion for a bit of horse back archery! After selling her and pilot husband Tim's medieval style family home known as the 'Sand Castle' in Perth's City Beach, she has plans to build an 'earth castle' on their 60 acre property north of Perth. But this is not your standard earth castle! Built into the sides of a man-made dam, there are plans for a drawbridge, turret and even a moate. With underground rooms and tunnels, and gothic features and finishes, the footprint is vast- nearly 2000 square metres in total. But Tani's endless modifications, coupled with fallout from the pandemic take a toll on the budget and the timelines- not to mention many of Tani's dreams for her medieval fortress!

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Steven and Theresa own a dated six-bedroom home which has been on the market for three years with three agents. They've lowered the price from $680,000 to $570,000 and can't really go lower. The market is flooded with more contemporary homes, meaning their house isn't currently very appealing to buyers.

programme.title: Love It Or List It Australia
programme.desc: Will Kristin and Di move to a new home with enough land for a big veggie garden and a brood of chooks or will Neale be able to reconfigure their current Queenslander home and make them stay put.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Emergency
programme.desc: Richard arrives at St George's after falling 3m from a ladder; Doreen is rushed to resus with a serious infection; five-year-old Connor John is in paediatrics with his dad after falling at home.

programme.title: Gogglebox UK
programme.desc: On the 'Box: Race Across the World, The Apprentice, Ripley, MasterChef, Hunted, Celebrity Bridge of Lies, The Assembly and BBC News (Hardest Geezer).

programme.title: Gogglebox UK
programme.desc: The Goggleboxers review This Morning, a Ministerial Broadcast by the Prime Minister, Hospital: Fighting Covid-19, After Life, Paul Hollywood Eats Japan, and Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

programme.title: Gogglebox UK
programme.desc: The Goggleboxers review After Life, Britain's Got Talent, The Andrew Marr Show with Michael Gove, First Dates Hotel, and Breakfast on Eurovision.

programme.title: Gogglebox UK
programme.desc: The Goggleboxers review First Dates, This Morning, Hunted, Tiger King, EastEnders, and The Queen.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Pete and Carina want to create a caravan site, but who will use it? James finds out about holiday home rental rates and Ioan builds on his newfound independence.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: This is an incredibly dated 1970s four-bed home on an elevated block with a steep driveway. This lovely family with two kids are preparing to downsize, but they seem slightly clueless as to why their house won't sell, and it's scaring off the buyers. It's been on the market for 14 months with a hopeless agent that they have replaced. They'll need to lower their expectations as they're currently asking well over the suburb average, even before the team renovates.

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The sewers celebrate Indian fashion with special guest judge Priya Khanchandani. Challenges include a Nehru jacket, an outfit from Calico and Madras Cotton, and evening wear inspired by the Sari.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers. It is vast, untamed, and home to some of the toughest to reach places on Earth. For the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, or braving a continent's worth of brutal bitumen and desolate dirt, this is a land that can destroy trucks and truckers in a heartbeat. But for the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again, for a season of dirt, dramas and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another fun packed episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie along with hilarious guest performer Keegan Michael Key. Highlights include the improvisers taking the chance to have fun with the personal belongings of members of the studio audience in a game called What's In the Bag?, tackling funerals, airplanes and ice cream flavours as Scenes From a Hat, donning wigs to act out a crazy family wedding, lying down to break out of prison in the always impressive Sideways Scene and Keegan giving Ryan a helping hand to get messy with Mexican food.

programme.title: Pawn Stars

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another hilarious episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie along with guest performer Jonathan Mangum and star guest Kyle Richards from Bravo's The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills. Highlights include Wayne Brady's unique take on video game Grand Theft Auto in the game Weird Newscasters, a crazy shootout in the mind-scrambling Sideways Scene and typically bizarre suggestions for what some weird props might be. programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?

programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another hilarious episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie along with guest performer Jonathan Mangum and star guest Kyle Richards from Bravo's The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills. Highlights include Wayne Brady's unique take on video game Grand Theft Auto in the game Weird Newscasters, a crazy shootout in the mind-scrambling Sideways Scene and typically bizarre suggestions for what some weird props might be. programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: With the departure of the original creative team, a fresh team of writers take over running the show. Bill Oakley and Josh Weinstein build on the legacy, expanding the world and exploring the side characters. To stay relevant, the Simpsons had to evolve; opening its doors to new creative voices, celebrity guest stars and even take on the big screen.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Remaining on the air for over 30 years is no small task yet the Simpsons has always managed to stay fresh and keeping up with the modern era. Even contract negotiations and corporate mergers never put the Simpsons' future into question. But what there's no doubt about is the impact the show has had on viewers, television and popular culture.

programme.title: Funny or Die Presents
programme.desc: In Lady Refs, tensions are heightened between Jablonsky and Kennedy after Dirkson's on-the-field injury. Body Boys: Legend of the Pipers tells of the most famous bodysurfing crew in the world.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Bringing viewers inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey, jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: In a show packed with unforgettable moments, the performers take the chance to have fun with the personal belongings of members of the studio audience in a game called What's In The Bag?, Colin Mochrie stands in front of some creepy reptiles in Newsflash and then acts as a Hollywood Director in an action packed Batman and Catwoman sketch. Star guest Maggie Q joins in the fun sending up her assassin persona in a hotel room scenario before getting increasingly Irish with Ryan in a drink and food-fuelled Helping Hands.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another hilarious episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie along with guest performer Nyima Funk and special guest, former Olympic gold medal winning gymnast Shawn Johnson. Highlights include Wayne Brady's brilliant songs about plumbing in rockabilly and Hawaiian ukulele style, the performers dream up typically bizarre suggestions for the use of some oddly shaped props, tackle tattoos and Harry Potter in the legendary Scenes from a Hat and a gangster interrupting his moll and her guest in the mind-scrambling Sideways Scene. programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: In Michigan, a late collector's home features a stockpile of awesome wheels. And a wide-eyed Mike comes up with a staggering six-figure offer - the largest in American Pickers history - to take home five ultra-rare motorbikes. Meanwhile, Danielle nails a shot-in-the-dark mission for rock'n'roll legend Jack White and then it's up to Robbie and Dave to transport a legendary recording trailer to its new home in Detroit.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Winds in excess of 48km/h batter Desert Hot Springs, and the buyers do their best to keep standing; Rene and Gunther dust off an old locker; Kenny steps up his game.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: Not All That Glitters Is Gourd
programme.desc: Back in the city of Orange, the Vegas Ladies arrive in vintage style, though not everyone agrees; Casey finds the chutzpah to pick up a good unit; Darrell tries to peddle his wares to Emily.

programme.title: Junior Taskmaster
programme.desc: Strap yourselves in because it's the first semi final. Our contestants meet Mark the Milkman for the first time, and perform poetry, while displaying varying levels of drama.

programme.title: Bookie
programme.desc: While Lorraine takes Walt on a spiritual business trip, Hector recruits Danny and Ray to help collect on a soccer bet from a notorious drug lord.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan and Charlie both wake up next to a weird woman, leading them to try to figure out what happened the night before.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Chelsea invites her college friend to stay at the house after she has a bad break-up.

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. Five truck owners will compete in a series of gruelling challenges that test their craftsmanship and driving ability to the max. Each episode features unique challenges that test trucks in a way that's never been seen before. The challenges culminate in The Green Hell, a three-mile obstacle course which includes challenges that send trucks flying off jumps, over a mountain of crushed cars and even through a snake-infested swamp. Helping them along the way are four experts in the off-road world - they coach, they build, they fix, but the one thing they can't do is drive. In the end, the truck who finishes is crowned the champion - if they can make it that far. In this episode, an epic line-up features a 2000 horsepower Dodge pick-up competing against a 1971 Land Rover. In a never-before-seen twist on the tug-o-war, the Silo Pulldown dumps a big surprise on the losers. And one challenge hits hard, forcing the medics to rush in.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: There's trouble in the wind for Kurt Shepherd who is struggling with a load causing oversized problems. For Johnny Dowell on the Tanami Track it's too hard one minute and too soft the next.

programme.title: WWE Raw Highlights

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Join Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald, Australia's own pickers, as they scour the country's back roads and barnyards in the quest to turn one man's trash into another man's treasure. Along the way we meet some of Australia's most authentic and colourful characters, whose stories reveal a rich Australian history, and what the pickers find will surprise and astound you.

programme.title: Spectacular Spider-Man

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: Spawn looks to uncover the truth about his identity and fulfil his destiny.

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: Spawn's nightmares reveal that Al Simmons' killer was his mercenary ex-partner, Chapel. CIA chief Jason Wynn tells Chapel to find a cache of stolen guns before they are uncovered and traced back to him.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: The respite doesn't last long; in September the men learn they're being airlifted to Holland as part of Operation Market-Garden, a joint American-British offensive that aims at securing quick access to Germany and ending the war by Christmas. Unlike Normandy, the daytime parachute drop is executed smoothly, and the company is greeted as heroes as they enter the town of Eindhoven after minimal resistance. All that changes when the company marches to Nuenen, where Easy is met by a brutal attack that ends with Compton wounded, a retreat ordered, and 'Bull' Randleman stranded in the town overnight. It's a sobering and bloody initiation to combat for the replacements.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As Winters labours over a report on the dike mission, Heyliger leads a rescue of British soldiers escaping from the besieged town of Arnhem. An anxious Winters worries about Easy (now one of three companies he helps command), especially after Heyliger is shot and wounded by a nervous sentry. After moving off the line to France, Nixon insists that Winters take a break and see Paris. Winters is haunted there by the memory of a young German he killed at close range on the dike. As he returns to the Company at Mourmelon, news comes of a massive German counterattack in the Ardennes Forest. Winters helps Easy race there to hold the line, his men ill-equipped for the cold and the battle ahead.

programme.desc: The respite doesn't last long; in September the men learn they're being airlifted to Holland as part of Operation Market-Garden, a joint American-British offensive that aims at securing quick access to Germany and ending the war by Christmas. Unlike Normandy, the daytime parachute drop is executed smoothly, and the company is greeted as heroes as they enter the town of Eindhoven after minimal resistance. All that changes when the company marches to Nuenen, where Easy is met by a brutal attack that ends with Compton wounded, a retreat ordered, and 'Bull' Randleman stranded in the town overnight. It's a sobering and bloody initiation to combat for the replacements.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As Winters labours over a report on the dike mission, Heyliger leads a rescue of British soldiers escaping from the besieged town of Arnhem. An anxious Winters worries about Easy (now one of three companies he helps command), especially after Heyliger is shot and wounded by a nervous sentry. After moving off the line to France, Nixon insists that Winters take a break and see Paris. Winters is haunted there by the memory of a young German he killed at close range on the dike. As he returns to the Company at Mourmelon, news comes of a massive German counterattack in the Ardennes Forest. Winters helps Easy race there to hold the line, his men ill-equipped for the cold and the battle ahead.

programme.desc: Set in Imperial Beach, California, the last great surf-break before Tijuana, this is a story about the Yosts: a family of surfers whose awesome athletic talents have seemed for generations to come with a curse attached. The Yosts' reign and reputation has been eroded by years of bad luck, addiction and hubris and, just as things are looking like they can't get worse, a stranger named John arrives, and the Yosts' banal existence is lifted into something profound, miraculous and, possibly, universal.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As German officers hand over their weapons, soldiers raid well-stocked wine cellars, snag souvenirs from residences, and endure one final tragedy when a replacement soldier cracks. Despite the fact that VE (Victory in Europe) Day has passed, Easy faces the prospect of imminent deployment to the Pacific Theatre. But as the men compare "points" to see who has earned enough to go home, word arrives that the Japanese have surrendered, ending the war and dissolving Easy Company. A memorable closing vignette tells what happened to Winters and some of his men after they returned to the States.

Complicating matters, a newspaper story by Ricardo Ramos paints an extremely unflattering picture of Priority Homicide, a public relations nightmare that's going to require some fancy footwork by Pope, Taylor and Brenda to set right.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: When a public defender is murdered, Cosgrove and Shaw are surprised to find Price at the crime scene. His involvement in the trial compromises the case.

programme.title: The Closer
programme.desc: Brenda's parents, Cliff and Willie Rae, visit Brenda, trying to get her to pin down her wedding date. When a murder investigation interrupts the visit, Brenda can't let go of the case and brings her parents along to investigate. Will Brenda slow down when the health of someone close to home is in jeopardy?

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The CSI team inspects a twisted trail of evidence when a saleswoman washes up on Rockaway Beach along with a dead shark.

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: When a guidance counsellor is murdered at a private school, the mayor orders Mac and his team to drop everything to find out what happened.

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A grizzly bear attack in the everglades puts suspicion on the hunting party, but things get more complicated once their hotel room is found.

programme.title: FBI: Most Wanted
programme.desc: After a prized Kentucky racehorse is stolen and its groomer is taken hostage, the team quickly learn that they may be the only ones who consider the young woman's safety a priority over the horse's.

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A wealthy real estate developer is murdered, and the investigation uncovers a complex web of deceit, involving his wife and her two identical triplet sisters.

programme.title: FBI: Most Wanted
programme.desc: While shopping, Barnes and Jess are caught in the middle of a mall shooting, with the exits rigged so no one can escape. Gaines, Hana and Ortiz try to help from the outside.

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The boyfriend of a punk rock singer is shot to death in his apartment, and the key to solving the crime may be his missing roommate.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.desc: The team investigates the disappearance of a congressional aide after a hit man saves her from two men posing as NCIS agents.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: When a young woman dies a slow and painful death from an untreated infection, Cosgrove and Shaw suspect she was under the influence of a powerful cult.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a non-commissioned officer is shot dead on the freeway, the NCIS team take on the investigation. When it appears that the victim was leading a double life, it throws the investigation in a new direction.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a Marine's wife is murdered live on the internet, the investigation reveals that two housewives were running a live internet sex site and are now both missing.

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: When a joint FBI/CIA/NCIS raid goes bad, the team suspects a mole. It turns out to be the work of a hacker with evidence leading to a 15-year-old girl. Realising the girl's hacking program has been stolen, Callen goes undercover.

programme.title: The Closer
programme.desc: The body of someone extremely overweight turns up in the trunk of a car - the body of a man who stole $3 million in diamonds from his own jewellery store. But the body is in such bad shape, it's impossible to determine the cause of death. When the team finally tracks down a possible witness, they get quite a shock.

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: As the search for the 'Cabbie Killer' continues, riding a cab gets very deadly. The team must figure out how to keep New York City safe.

programme.desc: A cab driver is found dead and at first Mac believes they may have found the 'Cabbie Killer', possibly killed by vigilantes. But bodies are still piling up and the killer takes Reed hostage.

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A gaming official accused of trying to dip into Indian casino profits is found scalped to death, and the discovery of a dead prostitute's little black book suggests that he's part of a larger plot.

programme.title: FBI: Most Wanted
programme.desc: The team hunts for a young man who is using napalm-style bombs to attack his targets.

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: While training a rookie police officer, Danny finds the dead body of a car thief in the trunk of one of the Ferraris she stole.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.desc: During the annual Contraband Days pirate festival, the team investigates the murder of a Navy captain who was searching for a valuable 200-year-old marble and gold fleur-de-lis.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Cosgrove and Shaw investigate the murder of a family physician whose outspoken politician wife suspects she was the intended target.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a six-year-old boy informs the NCIS team that his father was kidnapped, the team discover that the missing father is actually a Lt Commander working on a secret project named Honor.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ziva and Tony spend time together in a hotel as they go undercover, assuming the identities of hired assassins who are now dead. Gibbs soon discovers that his team is not the only one working the case.

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team along with Homeland Security investigate the death of a security guard who worked at a train yard and they soon discover that they're searching for a mastermind who may be planning to launch a terrorist attack on a train.

programme.title: The Closer
programme.desc: When a woman is found brutally raped and murdered in her home, all eyes turn toward the prime suspect, a known sex offender who retains a lawyer before he's even arrested. The attorney turns out to be a match for Brenda, and winds up manipulating Brenda and her squad.

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A man takes hostages during a failed attempt to rob a bank. The man demands a CSI to come in and prove he wasn't the person who fatally shot the bank manager.

programme.desc: Mac barely escapes death after his kidnapping and quickly goes to work trying to track down the location and identity of the bank robber.

programme.title: Cold Case
programme.desc: After bank robberies by thieves wearing Johnny Cash masks, Rush finds a link that may give new information on an old murder of a bank teller during an almost identical robbery.

programme.title: Cold Case
programme.desc: When a drug case is reopened, Valens reveals he had worked undercover on the high-profile case, and now he must face the demons that have been haunting him for eight years.

programme.title: FBI
programme.desc: After a suburban woman is kidnapped from her home, the team look into trysts on both sides of a seemingly perfect marriage; OA feels pressure from Mona to make changes in his career path.

programme.title: FBI
programme.desc: The team must track down a shooter targeting detectives from the same precinct and unit, while facing mounting tension from the NYPD.

programme.title: NCIS: Hawai'i
programme.desc: Tennant and her NCIS unit must take down a notorious team of international thieves using WWII explosives on armoured trucks before they complete their final big job and disappear.

programme.title: NCIS: Hawai'i
programme.desc: Kai goes undercover with one of Hawaii's oldest surf gangs when a petty officer, who's trying to help wayward kids find a new path in the Marines, is murdered.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ziva reunites with Gibbs after warning him of the danger he's in meanwhile, the rest of the team struggles to come to terms with the fact that their friend and former colleague is still alive. The NCIS squad investigates a string of attacks against veterans, a network of spy cameras hidden inside a motel, and the murder of an officer that was streamed online.

programme.desc: The team attempt to extract key information from a vulnerable 9/11 widow, Hannah Thompson, who is shocked that her new boyfriend is the manipulative leader of a terrorist group planning an attack.

programme.desc: When a businessman is found murdered, his surprising connection to Cosgrove's daughter helps the police make a break in the case.

programme.desc: Ray spites himself when he complains that Debra is too clingy and cuddly in bed.

programme.title: The Office
programme.desc: With Jim and Pam hoping to delay arriving at the hospital until after midnight so they can stay two full nights, the office staff do what they can to distract Pam after she starts having her first contractions.

programme.title: The Office
programme.desc: Pam and Jim find themselves making an embarrassing blunder after the birth of their baby. Michael sets up Erin and Kevin for a break-room lunch date, making Andy jealous.

programme.title: The Drew Carey Show
programme.desc: Drew forms a union for single employees when a storm forces families to leave work early. He later uses Mr. Wick's trauma to his advantage during a tornado.

programme.desc: Drew becomes more assertive but gets more than he bargained for when he and Mimi discover an affair at work. Meanwhile, Kate and Oswald secretly date.

programme.desc: Doug and his friends get an apartment above a Chinese restaurant in order to be away from the women in their lives.

programme.desc: While Jerry dates a dermatologist to help George deceive a new employer, Kramer is obsessed with a new meat slicer.

programme.desc: Elaine takes Jerry and George to India to upset a rival's wedding. Meanwhile, Kramer is the subject of an evil birthday wish.

programme.title: The Office

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: It's Doug's 40th birthday, and when Carrie's co-worker begins to question Carrie on whether Doug is freaking out or not about getting older and not accomplishing a whole lot, Carrie then begins to try to make Doug realise that he should start wanting to do more with his life. So Doug decides he wants to ride a roller-coaster that he was too afraid to ride when he was younger.

programme.title: The King of Queens

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: The existence of Santa Claus is explained to Louis by Alan and Walden.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden finds it difficult to get close to women when his foster son, Louis, is home. Meanwhile, Herb asks for Alan's permission to go on a date with Lyndsey.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Babies, Lies And A Resplendent Cannoli
programme.desc: Sheldon is upset at the recent death of science-fiction author Isaac Asimov. Dr. Linkletter is a fan and Dr. Sturgis is interested in his novels. Since Sheldon's late grandfather enjoyed the novels as well, Sheldon talks Connie into joining his book club with the two professors to read "Nightfall". Once the two men find out Dale dumped Connie, they flirt with her and both leave her several voicemails. Meanwhile, Georgie tells Dale about Mandy being pregnant; Georgie is the father and Mandy wants to keep it. Dale later urges Georgie to tell George. Missy has her own disastrous experience babysitting Pastor Jeff's baby, alarming Georgie. In the garage, Georgie finally tells his father the truth.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A God-Fearin' Baptist And A Hot Trophy Husband
programme.desc: George is shocked and quickly has Georgie tell Mary. Though upset, Mary and George note they did the same when Mary got pregnant with Georgie. Mary informs Connie who is able to convince Mandy to come to dinner with the Coopers; relating how she felt back when Mary got pregnant. The dinner is awkward, with Mary wanting the baby to be raised Baptist and wanting Georgie and Mandy to get married. Mandy and George both think that is a horrible idea while Georgie would be willing to marry her. Mary and George get into an argument about their own marriage. Mandy is thankful for Connie's support though. Mandy later rebuffs Georgie's declaration of love for her. Meanwhile, the truth is hidden from Sheldon and Missy. When they are sent to Brenda Sparks for dinner, Missy and Sheldon start to think George could be dying or their parents could be getting divorced. They later mistakenly think Mary is pregnant.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As Adam looks to Weird Science to launch Barry's epic senior year, Beverly pitches in to help Erica start her college career.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: A class project about Pops' World War II stories lands Adam in trouble. Murray orders Barry and Erica to learn how to live without their mother's help.

programme.desc: In an attempt to solve an international case, Jake and Rosa are forced to work with a pair of Swedish cops and things get very competitive, very quickly.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Uncle Sheldon And A Hormonal Firecracker
programme.desc: Mary and George are still bickering. After Connie tells Sheldon and Missy that Mary isn't pregnant, Missy figures out the truth about Georgie. Mary swears the twins to secrecy. Talking about it without specifics, Mary later feels judged by Pastor Jeff. Sheldon cannot hold it and tells an uninterested Dr. Linkletter, George tells Coach Wilkins, Mary tells Pastor Rob and Missy tells a girl at school. Georgie is upset everyone spread it around and Mandy is further angry with him. Georgie has a heartfelt moment with Missy who believes Georgie will be a good father. George is further irritated Mary bonded with Pastor Rob again over this and listens to him when George had earlier given essentially the same advice; but doesn't tell her why he dislikes Pastor Rob. When nobody attends her Bible Study, a sad Mary calls Pastor Rob who says he always has time for her.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: White Trash, Holy Rollers And Punching People
programme.desc: After Missy punches a boy in Sunday school for making fun of her family, Pastor Jeff insists Mary take a leave of absence from working at the church to the objection of both George and Pastor Rob. At home, George disagrees with Mary that Missy should be punished. Pastor Rob stops by and prays with Mary, but Missy comes home interrupting their alone time. Mandy tells Connie her family has disowned her for being pregnant and invites her to accompany her to her medical examination. At the clinic, Georgie showed up despite being uninvited and after some bickering was allowed to stay, while Connie offers to cover the cost of Mandy's prenatal care. George is upset that Mary saw Pastor Rob again in private and storms out of the door. After Sheldon informs President Hagemeyer of his family troubles, Mary snaps at President Hagemeyer over the phone. Missy hugs and supports her mother, later covering for George's absence to her brothers and asking God to bless Mary.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly looks to set an example for her feuding sons by reuniting Coach Mellor and his estranged older brother. Murray enlists Erica's boyfriend to keep her from having too much fun in college.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As Adam's campus visit exposes the truth about Erica's college experience, Murray and Beverly are at odds when she looks to start a new career.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Charles' ex-wife tries to interfere with his relationship with Genevieve; Amy asks Terry for a recommendation letter; Holt and Rosa turn to Gina for help with a case.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Captain Holt and Jake contract the mumps during an investigation and decide to be quarantined together. Rosa tries to help Boyle mourn and Terry cleans up Hitchcock and Scully's mess.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden may lose Louis when the social worker finds out that he and Alan aren't actually a couple, but Alan isn't ready to give up without a fight.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden joins a support group for adoptive fathers, and learns it's nice to be away from Alan.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden worries about Louis and the adoption process when Alan breaks up with the social worker in hopes of getting back together with Lyndsey.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine has tickets for a historical clothing exhibition but realises that she has no female friends. Jerry suggests she ask Susan. Kramer says that is the wrong move because George's worlds will collide. Jerry meets his pool guy outside a movie, then can't get rid of him.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry finds the number of a girl he wants to contact on Kramer's AIDS walk sponsor list. After finding out that her preferred method of birth control is only available in a limited supply, Elaine must decide whether her current boyfriend is worthy. George tells Susan the secret of the size of Jerry's jeans and that leads to a fight about all secrets; he tells her another of Jerry's secrets when they make up. Jerry finds out that George told Susan one of his secrets and decides he's out of the loop.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: When Deacon and Kelly buy a vacation home, Carrie is upset because they could have easily afforded it, but they were too cheap.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: After almost running over a chicken on the highway, Doug becomes more animal-sensitive and decides to become a vegetarian.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Uncle Sheldon And A Hormonal Firecracker
programme.desc: Mary and George are still bickering. After Connie tells Sheldon and Missy that Mary isn't pregnant, Missy figures out the truth about Georgie. Mary swears the twins to secrecy. Talking about it without specifics, Mary later feels judged by Pastor Jeff. Sheldon cannot hold it and tells an uninterested Dr. Linkletter, George tells Coach Wilkins, Mary tells Pastor Rob and Missy tells a girl at school. Georgie is upset everyone spread it around and Mandy is further angry with him. Georgie has a heartfelt moment with Missy who believes Georgie will be a good father. George is further irritated Mary bonded with Pastor Rob again over this and listens to him when George had earlier given essentially the same advice; but doesn't tell her why he dislikes Pastor Rob. When nobody attends her Bible Study, a sad Mary calls Pastor Rob who says he always has time for her.

programme.desc: Frasier's show is subjected to a focus group evaluation, and 11 of the 12 participants have nothing but rave reviews for his work. But one man just doesn't like him, throwing Frasier into a frenzy as he desperately tries to find out why.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: On the third anniversary of Frasier's radio show, Roz gives Frasier a cassette of their very first broadcast. At home, he falls asleep while listening to the tape, and has flashbacks of his first days back in Seattle.

programme.desc: Will is reluctant to introduce his ballet dancer boyfriend to Grace. Meanwhile, Jack suggests to his company that he design their Christmas window.

programme.title: The Changing Face of the Queen
programme.desc: This is a celebrity biography series that tells the incredible stories of those A-listers whose careers have endured across the decades. Hugely talented within their fields, they've also been inventive in how they've used their image to keep themselves in the spotlight. Through the prism of their ever-changing style, we reveal each star's amazing life story - the highs, the lows, the sex, drugs and rock and roll.

programme.sub-title: Asim Chaudhry, Russell Tovey, D. Mantle

programme.title: Call Me Ted
programme.desc: Ted buys the Atlanta Braves, challenges the networks, and launches SuperStation WTBS. His sailing career flourishes, and he creates CNN, the first 24-hour news network.

programme.title: Secrets of Penthouse
programme.desc: As Penthouse enters the 1990s, the huge success of the magazine has led Bob Guccione to embark on a series of costly investments, including cryogenics and a casino in Atlantic City which is eventually sold to Donald Trump. Bob's youngest son Nick persuades his dad to let him branch out into video production. But the venture doesn't last and Bob pushes Nick out of the company which takes an emotional toll on Nick. Meanwhile Bob's relationship with his Penthouse 'Pets' takes a dark turn when Anneka DiLorenzo, a former 'Pet', takes Bob to court, accusing him of exploiting her as a sex slave.

programme.title: Secrets of Penthouse
programme.desc: As Penthouse enters the 2000s, the magazine is in trouble. With sales falling, Bob Guccione pushes for the magazine to go harder against the advice of those around him, and Penthouse loses advertisers. Bob has pushed his three sons out of the business and taps his daughter Nina to help run the business as the age of the internet dawns. Adult superstars and Penthouse 'Pets' Jenna Jameson and Sunny Leone are forging their own paths in the adult entertainment business, becoming huge stars online. After the death of his long-time partner Kathy Keaton, Bob's debts continue to mount, and he is forced to sell his New York mansion and private art collection. In 2010, Bob passes away following an illness. His youngest son Nick visits Bob's grave for the first time.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell head to Levens Hall in the Lake District for the grand finale.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Asim Chaudhry, Russell Tovey, D. Mantle
programme.desc: In this fourth heat, nine artists are challenged to create a portrait of one of three celebrities: comedian Asim Chaudhry, and actors Doreen Mantle and Russell Tovey.

programme.title: Call Me Ted
programme.desc: Ted buys the Atlanta Braves, challenges the networks, and launches SuperStation WTBS. His sailing career flourishes, and he creates CNN, the first 24-hour news network.

programme.title: Secrets of Penthouse
programme.desc: As Penthouse enters the 1990s, the huge success of the magazine has led Bob Guccione to embark on a series of costly investments, including cryogenics and a casino in Atlantic City which is eventually sold to Donald Trump. Bob's youngest son Nick persuades his dad to let him branch out into video production. But the venture doesn't last and Bob pushes Nick out of the company which takes an emotional toll on Nick. Meanwhile Bob's relationship with his Penthouse 'Pets' takes a dark turn when Anneka DiLorenzo, a former 'Pet', takes Bob to court, accusing him of exploiting her as a sex slave.

programme.title: Secrets of Penthouse
programme.desc: As Penthouse enters the 2000s, the magazine is in trouble. With sales falling, Bob Guccione pushes for the magazine to go harder against the advice of those around him, and Penthouse loses advertisers. Bob has pushed his three sons out of the business and taps his daughter Nina to help run the business as the age of the internet dawns. Adult superstars and Penthouse 'Pets' Jenna Jameson and Sunny Leone are forging their own paths in the adult entertainment business, becoming huge stars online. After the death of his long-time partner Kathy Keaton, Bob's debts continue to mount, and he is forced to sell his New York mansion and private art collection. In 2010, Bob passes away following an illness. His youngest son Nick visits Bob's grave for the first time.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo sets sail from Heysham to the Isle of Man, where he discovers the horse trams of Douglas, the 19th-century secrets of the giant Laxey Wheel, and the Victorian history of Snaefell Mountain Railway.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo observes the amazing engineering feat involved in building the railway along Dublin's treacherous East coast, explores 19th-century crime and punishment in a Victorian jail, and finds out how the lions of Dublin Zoo changed the fortunes of the railways.

programme.title: Comedy Legends
programme.desc: From his fruitful beginnings on Saturday Night Live to his starring roles in Anchorman and Holmes & Watson, Barry Cryer takes a look at the Hollywood star's career.

programme.title: Europe's Waterways
programme.sub-title: France - From The Camargue To The Ardeche
programme.desc: Venturing upstream on Europe's waterways, the series discovers five regions of the continent from an unknown perspective and visits people who live from, on and with water. Rivers, canals, lake regions: they have shaped Europe's cultures for centuries - they are a place to live, transport arteries and sources of energy. Five journeys on watercourses broad and narrow lead to Italy, Germany, France, Poland, Holland and Belgium. Along the way: new projects for the future of water in Europe.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Directors and musicians discuss the secrets behind creating some of Lou Reed's best-loved music videos.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: A unique insight into the ideas and vision behind the music videos of BRIT Award-winning rock band Crowded House.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: A celebration of 20 years of Other Voices - Ireland's longest running music television series. Featuring Sam Fender, Sigrid, Dermot Kennedy, Villagers, Kay Young, Little Simz, John Grant, Orla Gartland and Fontaines DC amongst others.

programme.title: The Canary Islands
programme.sub-title: La Palma - The Galapagos Of Europe
programme.desc: Off the African coast in the middle of the Atlantic, underwater volcanoes raged for millions of years and created a volcanic archipelago: the Canary Islands. Each of them has its own face. This series tells of natural spectacles, unique natural areas and the formation of the volcanic archipelago.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The New Zealand South Island line scales the snow-capped Southern Alps, travels through Maori hunting grounds and finally reaches Christchurch, a city sitting on a volcano.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: In this series, historian Paul Murton sets out to experience life on Scotland's magnificent islands. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present, pointing to all that makes these islands quirky, surprising and beautiful.

programme.title: Phil Collins: One Night Only

programme.title: Sinatra: To Be Frank
programme.desc: The life of Frank Sinatra, as an actor and singer and the steps along the way that led him to become such an icon.

programme.title: Branson
programme.desc: The series premiere explores Branson's early years and the influence of his iconoclastic mother. As a dyslexic teenager, he leaves school at age 15 to create a magazine called Student, leading to a mail-order record business and eventually Virgin Records and Virgin megastores.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Directors and musicians reveal the ideas and inspirations that helped create some of American rock band ZZ Top's most memorable music videos.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Robert Elms discusses the amazing body of music video work from Bjork. Featuring interviews with directors Chris Cunningham, Michel Gondry and Spike Jonze, and videos All is Full of Love, It's Oh So Quiet and Bachelorette.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: He only lived to be 28 years old. Heath Ledger was a star that didn't really want to be one, opting for roles in small films and declining many major offers.

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael revisits his fourth travel series, Sahara with Michael Palin (2002), a four-episode trek round the Sahara desert.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The Cambrian Line starts in Pwllheli and travels 200km, taking in the fairytale village of Portmeirion, the seaside resort of Aberdovey and the ancient capital of Machynlleth.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton continues his odyssey, discovering the secrets of some of the most remote places in Europe. His first journey takes him to the largest island in Britain: the long island of Harris and Lewis. Paul tries his hand at weaving Harris tweed, meets the family behind the world famous Stornoway black pudding and uncovers the remarkable story of how a mysterious medieval chess set was discovered on the remote beach of Uig on Lewis.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Thirty miles from mainland Scotland, there is a chain of low-lying islands that face the full might of the Atlantic Ocean: the Uists and Benbecula. On these islands there seems to be more water than land as a spectacular patchwork of freshwater lochs and pools pockmark the landscape. But it's the unique ecology of these unspoilt islands that makes them so special. Paul's journey sees him try his hand at fishing for wild sea trout, uncover the fascinating story of how South Uist became a missile testing ground, and discover the secrets of the beautiful but deserted tidal island of Vallay.

programme.title: Taken Together: Who Killed Lyric And Eli
programme.desc: Drew Collins confronts someone who may link a suspect to the Evansdale murder scene.

programme.title: Casey Anthony: Where the Truth Lies
programme.desc: Casey Anthony revisits memories from the period when her daughter Caylee went missing. As she slowly pieces together clues, Casey finally admits what she suspects happened that fateful day.

programme.title: The Jinx: Life & Deaths Of Robert Durst
programme.desc: Susan Berman was the daughter of a famous Las Vegas mobster, Davey 'The Jew' Berman, who was a partner with 'Bugsy' Siegel in The Flamingo Hotel. Robert Durst's closest friend since they attended UCLA together, she had a mobster's mentality and matching code of ethics; people said she would do anything for a friend. Since Kathie Durst's disappearance two decades earlier, Robert Durst had been the subject of media and public scrutiny, and she had become his spokesperson to the media. Some say that Robert and Susan kept each other's secrets, and that if anyone knew what happened to Kathie, it would be Susan.

programme.desc: The Cambrian Line starts in Pwllheli and travels 200km, taking in the fairytale village of Portmeirion, the seaside resort of Aberdovey and the ancient capital of Machynlleth.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton continues his odyssey, discovering the secrets of some of the most remote places in Europe. His first journey takes him to the largest island in Britain: the long island of Harris and Lewis. Paul tries his hand at weaving Harris tweed, meets the family behind the world famous Stornoway black pudding and uncovers the remarkable story of how a mysterious medieval chess set was discovered on the remote beach of Uig on Lewis.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Thirty miles from mainland Scotland, there is a chain of low-lying islands that face the full might of the Atlantic Ocean: the Uists and Benbecula. On these islands there seems to be more water than land as a spectacular patchwork of freshwater lochs and pools pockmark the landscape. But it's the unique ecology of these unspoilt islands that makes them so special. Paul's journey sees him try his hand at fishing for wild sea trout, uncover the fascinating story of how South Uist became a missile testing ground, and discover the secrets of the beautiful but deserted tidal island of Vallay.

programme.title: Branson
programme.desc: The series premiere explores Branson's early years and the influence of his iconoclastic mother. As a dyslexic teenager, he leaves school at age 15 to create a magazine called Student, leading to a mail-order record business and eventually Virgin Records and Virgin megastores.

programme.title: Taken Together: Who Killed Lyric And Eli
programme.desc: Drew Collins confronts someone who may link a suspect to the Evansdale murder scene.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo meets the craftsmen behind the Victorian furniture trade, discovers how George Bradshaw helped save Britain's canal heritage, and sees Shakespeare as a 19th-century railway tourist.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo travels to the manufacturing centres of the Midlands. He learns how the railways helped to make Birmingham the pen-making capital of the world, hears the chilling tale of one of 19th-century Britain's most notorious murderers and samples the delicacies concocted in a Victorian kitchen at Shugborough Hall.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: Star Wars
programme.desc: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-winning film editor and ex-wife of George Lucas, sits down for her first-ever on camera interview and provides unique insight into the rise of Lucasfilm.

programme.title: Artist to Icon
programme.desc: Features interviews with Whitney Houston's The Bodyguard director Mick Jackson, musician Wayne Linsey, The Preacher's Wife producer Elliot Abbott, plus many more.

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: The musical fallout from the night disco died, a rock God breathes life into Tina Turner's career and we explore the otherworldly occurrences in Rick Rubin's recording studio.

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Willie Nelson gets over on the IRS, the overlooked band that may have invented punk music and we look into the Frank Sinatra song that has spawned a dozen murders.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: In this 'best-of' episode we get to observe the most unusual artistic techniques, experience the challenges of painting 'en plein air', and indulge in stunning British landscapes.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: It's discovered that Old Mr Grace plans to ask a member of the staff to marry him. When he calls for Mrs Slocombe, everyone assumes she's the one.

programme.title: The Sea Wolves
programme.desc: British intelligence officers come out of retirement to destroy German freighters spying on Allied shipping.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica records one of her novels for the blind. While at the recording studio she gets an invitation to the taping of a rock video.

programme.title: Gomer Pyle

programme.title: Maverick

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie hires a convict, via a work-release program, to be a mechanic at his garage.

programme.title: McHale's Navy

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: To keep their feet nice and warm, the British spend almost 723 million pounds a year on socks! Gregg Wallace visits a sock factory in Leicester that produces one and a half million socks annually. This atmospheric factory is a fascinating mix of Wallace and Gromit-style and high-tech machinery, with a close-knit team of highly skilled operators. Elsewhere in the episode, Cherry Healey finds out what causes smelly feet and pounds the pavements to test out which socks best tackle this stinky situation. She visits a cotton spinning factory in Manchester, whose high-tech machinery produces 4,200 miles of yarn every hour, and meets a revolutionary eco cotton supplier. Historian Ruth Goodman steps back into the 1980s, when socks were at the height of fashion. And she ventures further back to the Great War, when the Kitchener stitch helped to save the feet of British soldiers in the trenches.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: This signature challenge is to bake one family-sized savoury quiche with a filling of their choice. The technical bake is a pistachio frangipane tart with roasted rhubarb and raspberries.

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On
programme.desc: Jamie shows us how to make fresh pasta with three simple sauces from ingredients you have, and a veggie chilli that heroes store cupboard ingredients.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie is sharing four more mouthwatering recipes. His succulent lamb stew is so quick to prep, but with seriously deep flavours; and a fast and furious tasty warm lentil salad.

programme.title: Jamie's One-Pan Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie gets festive with a whole range of delicious recipes to make your Christmas dinner extra-special. From a salmon gravadlax starter to brand-new ways to do the turkey, roasties and Brussels.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off Christmas Special
programme.desc: A festive welcome awaits four more returning bakers Chetna from Series 5, Howard from Series 4, James M from Series 3 and Janet from Series 2. The judges have set three festive challenges, starting with a savoury signature to get the Christmas party started. In the technical, Paul has a gifted them a bread challenge that's not for the faint-hearted. The final challenge, the showstopper, is huge, as the judges lay the festive gauntlet and ask the bakers to make a trio of Christmas favourites. But who will hold their nerve and produce the perfect desserts?

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon shows us how to make his favourite street food dishes at home. Recipes include delicious prawn tostada, irresistible caramel salted popcorn and his son Jack helps make buttermilk fried chicken, a Ramsay family favourite.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie is sharing four more mouthwatering recipes. His succulent lamb stew is so quick to prep, but with seriously deep flavours; and a fast and furious tasty warm lentil salad.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: These soothing and much loved home comfort delights range from the very simple to the extremely decadent. Mary indulges her love of comfort food with homely recipes to warm the heart, including eggs Benedict Florentine (why make one when you can cook both), fall-off-the-bone slow-cooked lamb shanks and deliciously rich chocolate truffle pots. She invites Swedish fire-chef Niklas Esktedt to share the joys of cave-man cooking with her, and finds herself outside by an open fire enjoying his inventive venison meatballs with blackened celeriac and mouth-watering Swedish doughnuts.

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat Christmas
programme.desc: From gloriously pink cocktails to doughnuts, the festive season is upon us, and Nigella brings joy, warmth and kitchen table tourism with food.

programme.title: The Great Canadian Bake Off
programme.sub-title: Great Canadian Bake Off: Christmas Special (2023)
programme.desc: Four champion bakers return to The Great Canadian Baking Show Tent for the chance to earn a second cake plate as the Holiday Star Baker.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom shares his step-by-step method for creating mouth-watering venison pies. For dessert, he makes a pear and chocolate frangipane tart served with Chantilly cream.

programme.title: Jamie's One Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie offers a range of one-pan dishes: speedy smashed lemongrass chicken, sweet potato chili with homemade nachos, and a chocolate party tray cake with buttercream.

programme.title: Gordon, Gino & Fred Desperately Seeking
programme.desc: Gordon, Gino and Fred are getting together for another extraordinary culinary Christmas adventure. 2020 has been a bit bleak to say the least and the boys are keen to end the year with a bang. After Gino led them to the deserts of Morocco for last year's mis-adventure, Gordon is determined that they celebrate the season properly this time, so has arranged a boys trip away before the madness of a family xmas kicks in, to the ultimate winter wonderland and the official home of Santa. GGF are going to Lapland to experience a treasure trove of festive fare, to enjoy spectacular landscapes and meet Father Christmas in person. Standby for a unique Christmas cracker!

programme.title: James Martin's Christmas Comforts
programme.desc: James shares a stress-free plan for the big day, pulling together the perfect Christmas meal for a crowd of his friends. Special guest is Paul Ainsworth.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: Chef Michael van de Elzen and restaurateur Ganesh Raj have a battle on their hands to persuade this family addicted to takeaway food and energy drinks to eat well for less.

programme.title: The Great Canadian Bake Off
programme.sub-title: Great Canadian Bake Off: Christmas Special (2023)
programme.desc: Four champion bakers return to The Great Canadian Baking Show Tent for the chance to earn a second cake plate as the Holiday Star Baker.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Today, it's unicorn fever, as our cake makers are set the challenge of producing three very unique unicorn cakes. Sugar craft specialist Eloise, uses al the colours of the rainbow to make a stunning 10th birthday cake, compete with over 150 multi-coloured roses. Rosie hopes for a cake that rocks as she tackles a rocking unicorn cake for a special christening party. Taking five days to make and wrapped in a whopping 50 kilos of fondant, her biggest challenge will be hand sculpting the tail, mane, and saddle blanket. Molly conjures up a dappled delight, as she creates a 2 and a half foot wide multi coloured mystical unicorn, complete with a golden horn, edible flowers and butterflies, to surprise 8-year-old birthday girl, Maddison.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie is sharing four more mouthwatering recipes. His succulent lamb stew is so quick to prep, but with seriously deep flavours; and a fast and furious tasty warm lentil salad.

programme.desc: Jamie offers a range of one-pan dishes: speedy smashed lemongrass chicken, sweet potato chili with homemade nachos, and a chocolate party tray cake with buttercream.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Christmas Dinner
programme.desc: It's Tom's favourite meal of the year and with clever planning and a passion for making everything taste as good as it can be, the result is a wonderful festive table laden with amazing food to delight family and friends.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: This signature challenge is to bake one family-sized savoury quiche with a filling of their choice. The technical bake is a pistachio frangipane tart with roasted rhubarb and raspberries.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: A newly single dad with two opinionated teenage boys gets an overhaul as chef Michael van de Elzen and restaurateur Ganesh Raj step in.

programme.title: Jamie's Best Ever Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie's recipes for the perfect Christmas are simple and succulent turkey, tasty stuffing, golden roasties, luscious gravy, and all the veg. There are also puds galore: a winter pudding bombe, chocolate truffles, and mince pies.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: This is classic Mary at her best as she returns to school to celebrate her light, easy and delicious cooking. From a simple version of the classic kedgeree and some delightful breakfast drop scones, to a moreish chicken, avocado and bacon salad and deliciously easy beef burgers. Mary takes her inspiration from a visit to a groundbreaking primary school in South London who has built a secret garden where they teach their students how to grow vegetables and look after a brood of chickens. It doesn't stop there, as Mary joins two enthusiastic and skilful year five pupils in the teaching kitchen to make something deliciously fresh for their school lunch.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon shows us his favourite recipes for an Italian breakfast, lunch, and dinner. His son, Jack, gets down to work, helping to cook crostini, beef pappardelle, and sensational panna cotta with hazelnut brittle.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie's cooking chilli con carne meatballs, plus sticky kicking chicken, watermelon radish salad and crunchy noodles.

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat Christmas
programme.desc: From gloriously pink cocktails to doughnuts, the festive season is upon us, and Nigella brings joy, warmth and kitchen table tourism with food.

programme.title: Gino's Italian Christmas Feast
programme.desc: Spend Christmas with Gino and the D'Acampo family and friends. This one-off special is a celebration of Italian food and traditions, culminating with a feast of all of Gino's favourites.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Cake artist Michelle, must bring a legend to life for her next commission when she's tasked with creating a 2 feet tall fearsome dragon cake, complete with striking rice paper wings, as a surprise 50th birthday. Eloise gets fruity with her latest order, as she attempts to make a zebra striped cake, surrounded by ultra-realistic fruit shaped and flavoured mini-cakes for a music festival. Karisha tackles a very challenging wedding cake for her best friend, which sees her hand placing over 10,000 sugar gems onto a towering 4-foot tall cake, so each tier looks like it's bursting open like a 'rock geode', shimmering with white crystals.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory in rural Somerset that produces one million pots of yoghurt every 24 hours. He meets the herd of Friesian cows that provide the milk, sees how the milk is processed and has a bacterial culture added, and watches how the extraordinary packing process takes place. Meanwhile, Cherry Healey helps out with the UK's biggest blackcurrant harvest, trying out plant based alternatives to milk and visiting a factory that makes food-safe yogurt pots from 100% recycled material. Also, historian Ruth Goodman hops on board the story of the electric milk float and tackles the contentious origins of the cream tea.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: With nine bakers left, it's time to step up the sweet tooth for chocolate week. Matt Moran has chosen a testing French chocolate dacquoise for the Technical Bake.

programme.desc: Chef Michael van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj step in to show how a family can save a fortune and eat far more nutritious meals.

programme.title: Jamie's Best Ever Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie's favourite festive season food, from Nan's Boxing Day brunch to amazing jerk ham, pancakes, Asian salads, clementine jelly, and party drinks. Also, delicious ways to use leftovers, including turkey pie and Christmas pudding strudel.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: Mary embraces the British countryside with fresh and honest bucolic cooking inspired by what we grow on our farms and gardens - from a joyous pasta inspired by a classic French herb sauce, to a perfect way to partner asparagus and how to serve up the most scrumptious roasted vegetables. Mary makes her way through our vegetable rows and orchards, ending with her own inspired version of the delicious tarte tatin. To further explore the green fields, she takes a step back in time to journey on one of the Watercress Line's 19th-century steam trains, where she leaves the stove to help fire up the 300-ton engine.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking

programme.title: Jamie's Quick And Easy Christmas

programme.desc: Jamie Oliver provides an inspiring collection of traditional Christmas classics, alongside new ideas and fantastic party food.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: Mary has always adored entertaining and pulls out some of her finest classics that will leave any guest begging for the next invitation - be it a romantic meal for two or hosting a big meal for friends and family. From her divine Dover sole cooked to perfection, a genius way to make beetroot gravadlax and a sticky chicken recipe that will have them knocking down the doors, to an ambitious crispy roast duck and a simple but exquisite raspberry mousse. For a look at the extreme ways one can decorate to entertain, Mary travels to Leeds Castle with acclaimed florist Simon Lycett as he prepares for the Festival of Flowers. Bringing Henry VIII's banquet hall to life, he shows Mary that too much is in fact never enough.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon shows us some of the American-inspired dishes he loves cooking for his family at home. Recipes include eggs, bacon, and hash browns, superb caesar salad, and irresistible peanut butter and jam cookies.

programme.desc: Jamie cooks crispy camembert parcels, autumn salad and cranberry dip, plus golden chicken, braised greens and potato gratin.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits the Ercol factory in Buckinghamshire, an area associated with furniture making since the 19th century. Brits spend a staggering 300 million pounds each week on furniture, and Gregg is following the production of one of this factory's best sellers, the Windsor chair. Starting life as oak and walnut trees from European woodlands, they're cut, drilled, steamed, curved and sanded until they're ready for delivery to shops and homes. Meanwhile, Cherry Healey investigates how sitting too much could be very bad for our health, and she helps to manage a sustainable woodland at the Rushmore Estate in Wiltshire. And historian Ruth Goodman discovers how utility furniture made during the Blitz is still influencing the designs we buy today, as well as learning how a humble British carpenter went on to make the most expensive piece of furniture ever sold.

Michelle makes an edible work of art for her village's annual art's festival in the form of a 6 and a half foot tall lime tree cake, with 20 kilos of edible bark, and hundreds of hand coloured rice paper leaves. Nastassja tackles a 4 and a half foot tall wedding cake, embellished with fondant flowers and edible lace, which must also include traditional Kente fabric patterns in its design as nod to the couple's Ghanaian heritage.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: Mary returns to one of her most loved locations, Port Isaac on the Cornwall coast. Along with the brilliant local Chef Nathan Outlaw she explores some of the wonderful classics inspired by the great outdoors - even if the weather is against them! From her take on a delicious Bouillabaisse filled with some of the ocean's best offerings, to a freshly caught Mackerel bbq'd by the sea, and some rich and crispy Spatchcock Poussin marinated and cooked alongside it. As the home cook and Michelin started chef cook alongside each other it is obvious how inspired they both are by the coast and a shared love of simple, fresh, local food cooked well.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Shortcuts

programme.title: Gordon, Gino & Fred Desperately Seeking
programme.desc: Gordon, Gino and Fred are getting together for another extraordinary culinary Christmas adventure. 2020 has been a bit bleak to say the least and the boys are keen to end the year with a bang. After Gino led them to the deserts of Morocco for last year's mis-adventure, Gordon is determined that they celebrate the season properly this time, so has arranged a boys trip away before the madness of a family xmas kicks in, to the ultimate winter wonderland and the official home of Santa. GGF are going to Lapland to experience a treasure trove of festive fare, to enjoy spectacular landscapes and meet Father Christmas in person. Standby for a unique Christmas cracker!

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Malt loaf has been a popular teatime treat for more than 80 years; these days we get through a staggering 130 million of them every year. So to get to grips with how this sweet and squidgy cake cum bread is made, Gregg Wallace is rolling up his sleeves to get stuck in, following a production line of massive dough mixing, mind-boggling tin filling and intensely hot baking. Meanwhile, historian Ruth Goodman reveals the surprising story of a British baking company that cooked up the first usiness computer, as well as visiting Cambridgeshire to find out how wheat flour was ground the traditional way, until the Victorians demand for white bread brought about the demise of Britain's iconic windmills.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers Christmas Special
programme.desc: Christmas just wouldn't be the same without cakes, and as the decorations go up, 6 of our Extreme Cake Makers are hard at work pushing their creations to new seasonal sugary highs! Ben has a battle to create a 3-foot tall Naughty Elf Cake in time to delight revellers and dignitaries at Chester's Christmas Lights Parade. Hand carved from chocolate cake and sugar paste, it weighs in at a staggering 30 kilos. Molly must tackle not one but three animal firsts, as she sculpts a lemur, meerkat, and armadillo, in the hope of creating the perfect festive bake to celebrate Santa's arrival at an Exotic Zoo. Suzanne flexes her sugar crafting and story writing skills as she creates Father Christmas reading a gingerbread book, covered in 3D modelling chocolate figures, that tells the tale of how Santa got his first red coat.
