programme.desc: Ainsley's journey takes him to Wollongong, for the hustle and bustle of the Friday Foragers Market. There's an emphasis on organic food, and before long a juicy watermelon catches Ainsley's eye.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Cookbook
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver has been cooking Christmas for his family for 20 years. In that time, he's experimented with loads of different ways of cooking the classics. But this year, he wants to show us his ultimate recipes - the ones he's decided really are the very best for Christmas. These are the dishes he'll be cooking on the big day for his own family this year.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Ultimate Christmas
programme.desc: Much loved British home cook Mary Berry is joined by some famous friends for her ultimate Christmas feast, packed with tips to plan and get ahead for the big day.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Week three and it's bread week. The remaining bakers have a lot to prove with three tough challenges, set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: An Extra Slice
programme.desc: Jo Brand teams up with Jonathan Ross, Ravneet Gill, and Josh Pugh, to celebrate Bread Week, highlighting joyful bakes and supporting those in need.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Veggie Christmas
programme.desc: Tom takes us step-by-step through his meat-free menu, including his stunning 'Cheesemas' parcel, crispy potato cakes and white chocolate trifle.

programme.title: The Great Canadian Bake Off
programme.sub-title: Great Canadian Bake Off: Christmas Special (2023)
programme.desc: Four champion bakers return to The Great Canadian Baking Show Tent for the chance to earn a second cake plate as the Holiday Star Baker.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom shares his step-by-step method for creating mouth-watering venison pies. For dessert, he makes a pear and chocolate frangipane tart served with Chantilly cream.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Shortcuts
programme.desc: Jamie's recipes for a spectacular Christmas feast: the ultimate roast turkey, a show-stopping veggie centrepiece, and a stuffing to please both meat-eaters and vegetarians.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Shortcuts
programme.desc: Jamie celebrates leftovers and transforms them into delicious dishes for between Christmas and New Year: A vegetable tarte tatin, Brussels sprouts Caesar salad and a spectacular trifle.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: Using everyday ingredients, James serves up dishes that look a million dollars but won't break the bank. He is joined by special guest Jason Plato.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by chef Kenny Atkinson and they set out to sample the delights of Northumberland. There's a trip to a lobster hatchery too, before James cooks local speciality Singing Hinnies on another breathtaking beach.

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: Raymond Blanc has made his home in the heart of the English countryside. For the past 30 years he has been carefully curating his abundant kitchen gardens.

programme.title: Jamie's One-Pan Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie gets festive with a whole range of delicious recipes to make your Christmas dinner extra-special. From a salmon gravadlax starter to brand-new ways to do the turkey, roasties and Brussels.

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On
programme.desc: Jamie shares delicious recipes that use whatever ingredients you can get our hands on in these unprecedented times. He makes fish pie using ingredients from the freezer, and a classic bread dough with an added twist.

programme.title: Gino's Italy: Secrets of the South
programme.desc: Gino heads back to Puglia's capital Bari and the town of Cisternino.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon embraces vegetarian home cooking as he creates a fantastic meat-free breakfast, lunch and dinner guaranteed to win over the most committed of carnivores. Gordon's daughter Tilly helps him to make a delicious dinner to die for.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Cookbook
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver has been cooking Christmas for his family for 20 years. In that time, he's experimented with loads of different ways of cooking the classics. But this year, he wants to show us his ultimate recipes - the ones he's decided really are the very best for Christmas. These are the dishes he'll be cooking on the big day for his own family this year.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: This family own a cafe but mum can't cook. They spend a fortune on food, but eat out five times a week and when they do hit the supermarket, it's the kids making all the decisions.

programme.title: Jamie's Easy Meals at Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie invites us to join him and his family in Essex as they prepare for a slightly different Christmas. Jamie has come up with some creative and useful recipes.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: Mary unleashes some of her classic favourites that have moved in and out of fashion, but still have a much loved stronghold. Her take on a classic prawn cocktail, a warming deep rich onion soup and a divine Banoffee pie are too enticing not to try out at home. Mary also prepares a leek and onion quiche to add to her ploughman's lunch and who better to eat it with than traditional farmer Robert Sampson who insists they do a bit of ploughing with his horse-drawn ploughs before sitting down to enjoy it.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon embraces vegetarian home cooking as he creates a fantastic meat-free breakfast, lunch and dinner guaranteed to win over the most committed of carnivores. Gordon's daughter Tilly helps him to make a delicious dinner to die for.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Veggie Christmas
programme.desc: Tom takes us step-by-step through his meat-free menu, including his stunning 'Cheesemas' parcel, crispy potato cakes and white chocolate trifle.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory tucked away amongst thatched cottages in the village of Wollaston, Northamptonshire. It may be a tranquil setting, but this factory has been making some serious noise in boot making for 120 years, producing boots for policemen and pop stars alike.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete comes to a startling realisation while staying at TJ Miller's place: that the comic has a hard time 'turning it off'. Later, the two head upstate to Jess's yard sale, where Pete teams up with an unexpected ally to get back a prized possession.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: In an effort to win some stage time at a comedy club, Pete spends the night 'barking', handing out flyers to passers-by, on the streets of New York. He finds himself encountering disinterested locals, befriending some up-and-coming comics, engaging in a turf war with a rival barker, and getting a pep talk from a veteran.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: When Pete's parents decide to come to New York for his mother's birthday, Pete begs Jess to keep up the charade of their marriage. But an incredibly tense dinner reveals some harsh truths about their relationships.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: While guest appearing on Artie's podcast, Pete meets a sympathetic Sarah Silverman, who invites him to stay with her and her collection of stray comedians. At Sarah's suggestion, Pete gets a gig as a 'warm-up' comic, and ends up being tasked with saving the day.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete's warm-up gig on Rachael Ray's show gives him a natural high, but he comes crashing down after a surprise visit from Leif's wife, who pulls him back into the chaos of his separation from Jess.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: After attempt to cheer up Pete goes wrong, Artie tags along with Pete to a baptism. Artie gets insight into religion and God from a woman, and Pete runs into Jess which makes him question how much he is willing to sacrifice for a life in comedy.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete has a chance encounter with famed magician Penn Jillette, who shakes his faith and inspires a questioning Pete to try living with abandon for one night.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete decides to spend the day with Leif, who encourages him to be open to the universe. Their carefree attitude is challenged when the two men find themselves at odds and in trouble with the law.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: When Pete is abandoned by Artie, he is rescued by comedian Bill Burr, who takes pity on him and offers up his guestroom. Bill encourages Pete to toughen up and embrace his masculine side as these two become unlikely friends.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete tries to be supportive when a fellow comic at The Boston gets his big break. Later, Pete reconnects with Jess, who encourages him to find a healthy way to express himself.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete reunites with Ali, who takes him on a tour of New York's alternative comedy scene, where they encounter a wide array of comedians and venues over the course of one night.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete's decision to organise a secret benefit for an ailing comedy vet finds him scrambling to put on a good show and being forced to face some hard truths about Artie.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: On a trip to NACA, a comedy conference to book college gigs, Pete and Ali find themselves having wildly different experiences dealing with a very particular audience, and a recognisable third wheel.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: In an attempt to show Ali that he has a backbone, Pete enters a comedy-roast battle, but finds that being mean is harder than he thought. The fierce competition brings some uncomfortable truths to the surface, forcing Pete to examine his priorities in life.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: As his successful two-month college comedy tour comes to an end, Pete, donning a new look and confidence to match, takes a college-aged aspiring comedian, Jaboukie, under his wing. But Pete's return to New York finds him having an awkward reunion and a big audition at the Comedy Cellar that could change everything.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Feeling like his time away from NY has taken him out of the scene, Pete takes an offer to perform at a Jewish show where Elon Gold and Modi question Pete's impulsive behavior with a woman he met earlier that day.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete falls headfirst into a relationship with his new girlfriend Kat, who encourages him to be more aggressive in his career. On the night of their three-week anniversary, things amp up after a visit to a sex shop and a comedy show that morphs into a night all about Pete.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete travels to New Jersey with Jason to do a weekend of shows at a big corporate comedy club and is surprised to find his ex Ali staying at the comedy condo with them. Jason's misogynistic act and point of view causes Pete to question his path in comedy.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: During a tour stop, Pete pays a visit to his parents and introduces them to Kat, who notices the uncomfortably close relationship he has with his overbearing mom. As he tries to keep the peace, Pete grows increasingly trapped between the two most important women in his life.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete and Kat attend a viewing party for Ali's late-night spot on Seth Meyers. Tensions mount as Ali is nervous about how her set went and Kat becomes jealous of Pete's friendship with his ex.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: While enjoying the perks of being on a Christian comedy tour, Pete feels busted when he runs into NY Cellar comic Jessica Kirson in Charleston. After ghosting Kat while on the road, Pete finally sits down to talk about their relationship.

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: Pete jumps at the opportunity of a lifetime to open for John Mulaney at Town Hall, but the gig leaves him in an awkward situation.

programme.title: Crashing

programme.title: Crashing
programme.desc: While considering his next move, literally and figuratively, Pete is put on the spot when Artie enlists his help in keeping him clean and sober during a road show in Albany with co-headliner TJ Miller. programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: At a bohemian party, Jemaine falls for Sally (who used to date Bret), but Bret spoils the moment. Meanwhile, Murray shares his two-pronged plan for the band.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Desperate for cash, Bret and Jemaine get jobs as human billboards to make ends meet. However, it interferes with the bands gig at a travel expo so Murray and Jemaine replace Bret with a cassette tape.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Bret loses Jemaine's trust after Bret abandons him when they get mugged by a gang of two. To regain his trust Bret must retrieve Jemaine's camera phone - a phone with a camera taped to it.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Murray is perturbed when Bret cancels a band tour to be with his girlfriend Coco. Jemaine accuses Coco of being Yoko-esque and threatens the future of the band.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: After Jemaine reconnects with Sally, Bret decides that he wants to pursue her as well. However, when they both show up at her birthday party to woo her with their homemade presents, they are in for a shock.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Upset when Murray says he's too small during a band photo shoot, Bret decides that Jemaine can help him get over his funk. Meanwhile, Murray pushes the band to let an audio greeting card company use their robot song.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Dissed by a rude fruit vendor, Bret and Jemaine concoct an animated music video in retaliation. Meanwhile, Murray falls for a leggy blond tech-support woman at his office.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Bret and Jemaine pick up two women at a croissant shop but end up with very different dating experiences. Meanwhile, Murray makes an album deal for the band.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Using the band's emergency fund, Murray organises a warm-up tour in preparation for a big gig in Central Park. At an airport bar, the boys get hooked by some mermaids from a women's water-polo team.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: The Conchords triple their fan base after playing a local world music jam, but Mel is suspicious of the two new fans' motives.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Bret and Jemaine enlist a semi-professional actor to cheer up Murray after a disappointing gig.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Murray foists a new bongo-playing band member on Bret and Jemaine, triggering angry dances and mutant half-bands.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: After rejecting Murray for spending too much time with another band, Bret and Jemaine find success managing themselves and land a deal to record an ad jingle.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Bret and Jemaine's fortunes unravel after Bret buys a new tea cup. With emergency band funds unavailable, Jemaine takes desperate measures in hopes of supplementing their income.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Bret organises a gang to protect himself from rappers he dissed in a song. Murray and Jemaine confront a group of Aussie bullies, and Mel paints a portrait Jemaine can't get rid of.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.sub-title: Murray Takes It To The Next Level
programme.desc: Murray tries to elevate Bret and Jemaine on his friendship graph, bringing his questioning pal Jim in on the plan, and Mel demands an apology from Bret for inappropriate dream behaviour.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Jemaine starts dating an Australian, to Bret's dismay and inconvenience.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.sub-title: Love Is A Weapon Of Choice
programme.desc: Bret and Jemaine both fall for a woman whose missing dog has epilepsy, triggering a duel, a canine benefit, and a couple of new tunes.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Foiled by the White House, Murray arranges a presidential meeting for the visiting Prime Minister of New Zealand, and a tribute gig ends with Jemaine getting mixed up with an Art Garfunkel fanatic.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Trying to look cool for a gig at the grand opening of New York's one-block New Zealand enclave, the Conchords end up getting hooked on hair gel.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Bret enlists Jemaine and Dave to help him try to land a girlfriend, and Murray regrets making Greg his scapegoat.

programme.title: Flight of the Conchords
programme.desc: Unable to pay off their back rent, Bret and Jemaine move in with Mel and Doug, and Murray looks to cash in on a stage play about the Conchords' travails.

programme.title: Flight Of The Conchords: Live
programme.desc: New Zealand music-comedy savants Bret McKenzie and Jemaine Clement showcase their unique ability to mix irreverent humour with clever original songs, performed on guitars and piano, among other instruments.

programme.title: Insecure

programme.desc: Pulling up to her 10-year college reunion in style as an alumni panellist, Issa's self-doubt quickly surfaces, while Molly, still reeling from her break-up, struggles to get out of her own head.

programme.title: Insecure

programme.title: Insecure
programme.desc: Still adjusting to new-found responsibilities, one of Issa's exes finds himself torn between two places, while an important woman in his life remains wary of his ability to be fully present back home.

programme.title: Insecure
programme.desc: Issa and her new man tread awkward waters at a work-related social event. Meanwhile, Molly juggles multiple suitors, and Issa plays defence against online attacks about her business.

programme.title: Insecure
programme.desc: A family emergency finds Molly stretched thin between taking care of loved ones, staying on top of work, and zeroing in on her own perceived shortcomings. Issa and her new man grow closer than ever.

programme.title: Insecure
programme.desc: A distracted Issa mulls over work and love as she stumbles through the consequences of her falling out with a colleague.

programme.title: Insecure
programme.desc: A long overdue girls' night filled with self-reflection and real talk puts things into perspective for Issa and her friends.

programme.title: Insecure
programme.desc: At a crossroads in her career, Issa weighs the pros and cons of two viable paths while simultaneously contemplating an important personal next step.

programme.title: Insecure
programme.desc: At a friend's going away party, Issa navigates unresolved feelings. After spending the day letting loose with her new man, Molly opens up about her fears.

programme.title: Insecure
programme.desc: As she and her friends try to make time for each other no matter what, Issa looks back on all it took to get to where she is today.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Grappling with the loss of her sister, Sam begins her journey to discover herself and a community of outsiders who don't fit in but don't give up, showing that finding your people is possible, anywhere, somewhere.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Sam begins to bond with fellow members of choir practice, but her burgeoning friendship with Joel is threatened over competing outlooks on their future in Kansas.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Sam and Joel embark on a covert mission to uncover the source of her brother-in-law Rick's sudden windfall. Choir practice provides Sam with the courage and clarity to share mounting concerns.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: After Pastor Deb asks for an impromptu meeting, Joel prepares for the worst. With money tight, Sam gets creative in sourcing a solution for the upcoming corn harvest.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: With a tornado looming, Sam prepares for a different kind of storm as she shares the truth about Rick with Tricia. After impulsively adopting a dog, Joel realises he wants more out of life.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: After a surprising update concerning her recent job performance, Sam must evaluate her next step. Meanwhile, Tricia and Charity's working relationship grows complicated.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: In her quest to reinspire Joel, Sam recruits a small team to help and reveals a new potential location for choir practice. Later, on a purple party bus, Sam realises just how far she's come.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: While he rents out his house, Joel and Sam revel in the highs of their temporary living 'situationship' and the lows of cleaning up after strangers.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: At poker night, Fred throws Sam, Joel, and Tiffani a curveball. Meanwhile, Sam attends a former voice teacher's concert in hopes of becoming her student again with unforeseen consequences.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: As Mary Jo's behaviour worsens, Sam and Tricia grapple with the difficult decision of escalating her care.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: After accidentally blowing his own cover stalking Brad, Joel opens up about his personal crisis stemming from Fred's upcoming wedding.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: While celebrating Tricia's birthday, Sam is blindsided by surprising new information about Holly. As Charity's lies continue to affect Tricia's social life, Joel makes a move with Brad, which leads to a misstep with Sam.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Distancing herself from Joel, Sam concentrates on helping Tricia with her blossoming pillow business - which soon faces sabotage from Charity. Meanwhile, Fred attempts to play peacemaker with Sam and Joel.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: After reuniting with Joel at a funeral, Sam takes stock of herself and her actions - and decides to make things right with her best friend.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Sam, a true Kansan on the surface, struggles to fit her hometown's mould. Grappling with loss and acceptance, singing is her saving grace and leads her on a journey to discover herself.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Overwhelmed by the amount of change happening in the lives of her loved ones, Sam is hit especially hard by a friend's brutally honest comment; Sam helps Brad with a special task.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Tricia shakes things up at the country club; Joel and Brad navigate their new normal; Sam finally sees a doctor.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: An unexpected issue arises on Sam and Tricia's trip to Kansas City for a trade show. Joel reluctantly attends Brad's bible book club and runs into a complicated figure from his past.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Surrounded by her sister and chosen family on Thanksgiving, Sam gains a better appreciation of what it means to love.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.sub-title: As Much As I Like Not Feeling?
programme.desc: Joel finds himself feeling unstable after an accident; Sam realises her deepest insecurities as she goes on a date with Iceland.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Sam always feels like an outsider in her hometown Kansas. However, she later finds a community where people like her gather amidst their struggles in life.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Attending an elaborate launch party, Richard and his computer programmer friends, Big Head, Dinesh and Gilfoyle, dream of making it big. Instead, they're living in a communal Hacker Hostel.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Peter Gregory demands a formal business plan for Pied Piper. Meanwhile Richard hires Jared, a former Hooli employee and they set about trying to trim the fat.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: While Gavin Belson begins to hype Nucleus, a competing compression platform, Richard learns that the name Pied Piper is already registered to a sprinkler company, forcing him to negotiate.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: At a toga party thrown by Peter, Richard drunkenly promises to make Erlich a board member - a decision he regrets the next morning.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Erlich convinces a graffiti artist to create Pied Pipers logo, with controversial results, and Jared tries to make the company more efficient.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard feels threatened when the team hires The Carver, a hacker with a notorious reputation, to help with Pied Piper's cloud.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: At TechCrunch Disrupt, Richard is distracted by a girl he dated briefly, and Erlich's connection with a judge threatens Pied Piper.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Poised to compete at TechCrunch Disrupt, the guys of Pied Piper become worried after an impressive presentation by Gavin Belson.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: After taking the tech world by storm at TechCrunch Disrupt, Richard and his Pied Piper team look ahead to a bright future, but are immediately soured by petty revenge plans from arch rival, Nucleus.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard and the guys struggle to find a backer as they face legal and financial woes. Jared uses the BRO2BRO app to try and help get them out of their mess.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard mulls Gavin's proposal, but also considers a pitch from billionaire Russ Hanneman. Monica learns unexpected news about Richard's deal with Hooli, and Gilfoyle and Dinesh go to extremes to get what they want.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard and Erlich butt heads over a potential employee. Dinesh and Gilfoyle become suspicious that someone they recommended for the company will be receiving more money than them.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Gavin's interference causes Pied Piper to struggle with securing their expansion, a neighbour threatens the team's security, and Dinesh and Gilfoyle are again questioning whether to embark on a solo mission.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: The Pied Piper crew races to show up Hooli after Nucleus' UFC livestream debut is botched. Erlich introduces them to the godfather of Homicide energy drink and former "mentee" Double-A to get some PR advice.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Russ becomes distracted by financial news and Richard faces the prospect of folding. Meanwhile, Dinesh tries wooing a woman online and Gavin revels in Nucleus' failure.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard causes trouble when he sympathises with a rival, making him paranoid about security. Meanwhile, Pied Piper, Gavin, and Intersite all face massive losses, undoing any progress made in recent weeks.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Erlich wants to testify when Pied Piper and Hooli enter a negotiation, but Richard worries that his rival's claims could be legitimate. Jared, Dinesh and Gilfoyle debate a philosophical theory.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: The guys wait anxiously to hear whether it's life or death for Pied Piper. Meanwhile, Erlich sends a message to the real estate buyers and Dinesh's incompetent coding works out better than expected.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: After being unceremoniously fired, an angry Richard faces a tough decision: whether to accept the diminished role of CTO, or leave Pied Piper behind for good. Meanwhile Erlich takes a shine to Lauries new choice of CEO, Jack Barker, while Dinesh and Gilfoyle weigh their options in Richard's absence. At Hooli, Gavin tries to improve his image by admitting failure, and Big Head gets wind of some major changes.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: While Pied Piper's new and improved offices and perks impress Dinesh and Gilfoyle, Richard worries that his vision has been compromised, especially after hearing from Jack's newly-hired sales team. Jared faces issues evicting renters from his condo, while a frustrated Erlich struggles to get rid of Jian-Yang. Gavin suggests a controversial move at Hooli.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Facing the bleak future of Pied Piper's new business agenda, Richard tries to appeal to Laurie for help. Gilfoyle opens himself up to recruiters' pursuits. Dinesh's latest flashy purchase brings him unwanted attention. After stumbling upon information about the competition, the guys decide to work up a master plan to covertly force Jack's hand.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Forced to complete an uninspiring project, the guys struggle to perform at a lesser level, ending up with a product that's more successful than they intended. Erlich discovers he's facing Hacker Hostel competition from a familiar face; Monica takes a stand at a board meeting. Convinced of Pied Piper's threat, Gavin makes a decision to double down on Nucleus.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Nervous about Laurie's next steps, Richard takes matters into his own hands, letting his ego get the better of him during an interview. While cleaning house in an attempt to cut costs, Dinesh, Gilfoyle and Jared misplace a very important piece of hardware. Laurie reveals her true intentions after inviting Monica out for a drink. Erlich faces unexpected pushback while trying to sell Big Head on his plans for their future.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard finds himself in his element while leading a global staff of engineers, but his budding relationship is threatened by his coding neuroses. Dinesh takes an interest in a foreign coworker, but worries about a face-to-face meeting. Big Head and Erlich's launch party hits a snag, or two, while Jared returns to the dating scene.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: When the guys decide to release the beta version of Pied Piper, they receive an unexpected response. With a limited number of invites each, Dinesh worries about his lack of friends.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Erlich struggles to come clean to Richard, who is forced to make a choice between their friendship and the company's future.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Upon discovering surprising stats, Richard attempts to bridge the gap between Pied Piper and its users, leading Jared to take drastic measures to hold everything together.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: With Pied Piper's future in question, Erlich's publicity success leaves Richard in a moral quandary, just as Dinesh's video-chat app gathers speed.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard mulls Gavin's proposal, but also considers a pitch from billionaire Russ Hanneman. Monica learns unexpected news about Richard's deal with Hooli, and Gilfoyle and Dinesh go to extremes to get what they want.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard and Erlich butt heads over a potential employee. Dinesh and Gilfoyle become suspicious that someone they recommended for the company will be receiving more money than them.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Gavin's interference causes Pied Piper to struggle with securing their expansion, a neighbour threatens the team's security, and Dinesh and Gilfoyle are again questioning whether to embark on a solo mission.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: The Pied Piper crew races to show up Hooli after Nucleus' UFC livestream debut is botched. Erlich introduces them to the godfather of Homicide energy drink and former "mentee" Double-A to get some PR advice.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Russ becomes distracted by financial news and Richard faces the prospect of folding. Meanwhile, Dinesh tries wooing a woman online and Gavin revels in Nucleus' failure.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard causes trouble when he sympathises with a rival, making him paranoid about security. Meanwhile, Pied Piper, Gavin, and Intersite all face massive losses, undoing any progress made in recent weeks.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Erlich wants to testify when Pied Piper and Hooli enter a negotiation, but Richard worries that his rival's claims could be legitimate. Jared, Dinesh and Gilfoyle debate a philosophical theory.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: The guys wait anxiously to hear whether it's life or death for Pied Piper. Meanwhile, Erlich sends a message to the real estate buyers and Dinesh's incompetent coding works out better than expected.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: After being unceremoniously fired, an angry Richard faces a tough decision: whether to accept the diminished role of CTO, or leave Pied Piper behind for good. Meanwhile Erlich takes a shine to Lauries new choice of CEO, Jack Barker, while Dinesh and Gilfoyle weigh their options in Richard's absence. At Hooli, Gavin tries to improve his image by admitting failure, and Big Head gets wind of some major changes.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: While Pied Piper's new and improved offices and perks impress Dinesh and Gilfoyle, Richard worries that his vision has been compromised, especially after hearing from Jack's newly-hired sales team. Jared faces issues evicting renters from his condo, while a frustrated Erlich struggles to get rid of Jian-Yang. Gavin suggests a controversial move at Hooli.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Facing the bleak future of Pied Piper's new business agenda, Richard tries to appeal to Laurie for help. Gilfoyle opens himself up to recruiters' pursuits. Dinesh's latest flashy purchase brings him unwanted attention. After stumbling upon information about the competition, the guys decide to work up a master plan to covertly force Jack's hand.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Forced to complete an uninspiring project, the guys struggle to perform at a lesser level, ending up with a product that's more successful than they intended. Erlich discovers he's facing Hacker Hostel competition from a familiar face; Monica takes a stand at a board meeting. Convinced of Pied Piper's threat, Gavin makes a decision to double down on Nucleus.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Nervous about Laurie's next steps, Richard takes matters into his own hands, letting his ego get the better of him during an interview. While cleaning house in an attempt to cut costs, Dinesh, Gilfoyle and Jared misplace a very important piece of hardware. Laurie reveals her true intentions after inviting Monica out for a drink. Erlich faces unexpected pushback while trying to sell Big Head on his plans for their future.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard finds himself in his element while leading a global staff of engineers, but his budding relationship is threatened by his coding neuroses. Dinesh takes an interest in a foreign coworker, but worries about a face-to-face meeting. Big Head and Erlich's launch party hits a snag, or two, while Jared returns to the dating scene.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: When the guys decide to release the beta version of Pied Piper, they receive an unexpected response. With a limited number of invites each, Dinesh worries about his lack of friends.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Erlich struggles to come clean to Richard, who is forced to make a choice between their friendship and the company's future.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Upon discovering surprising stats, Richard attempts to bridge the gap between Pied Piper and its users, leading Jared to take drastic measures to hold everything together.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: With Pied Piper's future in question, Erlich's publicity success leaves Richard in a moral quandary, just as Dinesh's video-chat app gathers speed.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard struggles to embrace Pied Piper's new video-chat app, Erlich faces resistance, and Gavin seethes at a slight.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Dinesh and Richard butt heads over PiperChat, Erlich's interest is piqued by Jian-Yang's new app, and Gavin grows paranoid.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard works tirelessly on his next big idea; Monica sets a trap; Dinesh goes on a date; Big Head heads back to school.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard's new alliance worries Jared, Gilfoyle considers whether to put his pride aside, and Erlich confronts Jian-Yang about his app.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard deals with an unexpected interloper; Dinesh searches for a way out; Monica considers her options.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard looks outside the tech bubble for support, Erlich searches for a new endeavour, and Jared tries to keep the peace between Dinesh and Gilfoyle.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard takes on a patent troll; Gilfoyle battles a smart fridge; Jared embraces multiple identities; Erlich tries to hang with a new crowd.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard ponders a deal with the tech world's latest 'it' boy, while Jack faces setbacks.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: The Pied Piper guys try to pull off a stealth plan at Hooli-Con; Erlich takes a trip to get his mojo back.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard finds himself in a web of lies; Jared plans his exit; Jack bets big; Gavin plots a comeback.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard faces new challenges as a leader as Pied Piper levels up, and Gavin worries about being antiquated.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard faces challenges while trying to unite new employees, Jian-Yang goes to court, and Gavin is questioned over his signature.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard's infatuation with another company's COO causes friction with members of Pied Piper, and Dinesh gets a new roommate.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Attempting to woo a gaming company to PiperNet, Richard inadvertently angers one of his prized allies. Dinesh deals with a betrayal. In preparation for a big launch, Gavin leaves his underlings with a cryptic message. Jared gets inside information from Big Head.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Overshadowed by Jared in an on-camera interview, Richard's confidence wavers further when Laurie and Monica force him to work with a new artificial-intelligence company, Eklow. Gilfoyle worries about the prospect of introducing AI into Pied Piper. Dinesh makes Jared self-conscious ahead of a second interview. Gavin questions his future beyond Hooli.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard decides to help when Laurie gets in a jam, but his lack of emotional discipline threatens to backfire on Pied Piper. Gavin tries to make a deal with a stubborn partner while abroad. Dinesh relishes a rare win. Jared bonds with a surprising figure.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: As the Pied Piper guys prepare to close on their Series B funding, Richard receives unsettling news. Dinesh competes with a co-worker, while Jared keeps a close watch on Richard's new assistant.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: PiperNet finds Monica suspicious of an early success, and the team must race against the clock as their future is threatened. Realising he's made more enemies than friends, Richard makes a surprising move.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard discovers his promise to keep Pied Piper free from collecting user data is under threat, while Jared finds himself missing his role as Richard's go-to guy.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard meets a potential investor, while Gilfoyle butts heads with HR. Gavin explores a leaner Hooli's future.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: The Pied Piper team races to close a major deal, while Dinesh considers being a better person. An angry Jared reluctantly helps Richard.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: When Richard's authority is threatened by his former Hooli manager, Dinesh has just the solution. Jared learns some hard truths about his birth parents.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard fumes at Gavin's new ethical stance, Dinesh's trip turns into a nightmare, while Gilfoyle and Monica work on their peer review scores.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard, Dinesh, Gilfoyle and Jared face their greatest challenge yet as a massive event tests PiperNet's capabilities, while Laurie's unexpected presence makes them wary of sabotage.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard and the Pied Piper guys finally see the fruits of their labour, but realise that a bigger company means much bigger problems.
